comments.author: @imteeaztourabaly7717
comments.text:
Thank you for this amazing discovery video. I'm proud to be Mauritian and proud to call this beautiful island home.

However, one thing really bothered me and found it unfair and disappointing. The statement "They make love in public" is simply not an representation of Mauritian people. We are a respectful, modest, and multicultural society that values privacy and intimacy.

I truly hope this can be corrected, as the rest of the video beautifully showcases our island, our people, and our rich cultural heritage.

Thank you

comments.author: @GlobeExperiences2026
comments.text: Mauritius is truly a hidden jewel in the Indian Ocean! 🇲🇺 This video captures the incredible reality of life in Mauritius—from being one of the safest and wealthiest nations in Africa with no standing army to its vibrant, multicultural harmony. It’s fascinating to see how high quality of life, breathtaking tropical landscapes, and peaceful living come together here. A complete eye-opener for anyone looking for a true island paradise or expat destination!

comments.author: @davecottom4250
comments.text: I have been to Mauritius and it is absolutely fantastic.  I stayed in Trou de Deuce with a friend so cannot comment on hotels except to say the price of food and drink is a lot cheaper in the local bars and restaurants.  The people are great the prices locally are fair but you can bargain. All in all I would definitely return.

replies.author: @AmazingGlobalDiscoveries
replies.text: Mauritius truly sounds even more special when explored through local bars, restaurants, and everyday life rather than only the resort areas. I’m glad to hear the people were welcoming and the local prices felt fair. Trou d’Eau Douce is such a beautiful part of the island too. What was your favorite place or meal during your stay?

comments.author: @telehappy_usa
comments.text: Amazing video! Mauritius is now on my travel bucket list. Which part of the island would you recommend visiting first for someone going there for the first time?

replies.author: @AmazingGlobalDiscoveries
replies.text: Thank you so much! 😊 For a first visit, I’d recommend starting in the southwest around Le Morne and Chamarel. You’ll experience beautiful beaches, dramatic mountain scenery, waterfalls, and some of the island’s most memorable nature. Port Louis is also worth visiting for the market, food, and local culture. I hope Mauritius makes it from your bucket list into your travel plans soon!
